entities.name: Resume
entities.context: Described as showing only surface information (workplace, title, tenure) but nothing about thinking, behavior under pressure, or relationships

entities.name: Competence
entities.context: What companies should be hiring for but aren't; distinguished from appearance of competence

entities.name: Appearance of competence
entities.context: What companies actually hire for; V argues this can be faked

entities.name: The Fraud Problem
entities.context: V's framing of the fake candidate issue in technical hiring

entities.name: Fake candidates
entities.context: Fraudulent actors applying to technical roles, not just exaggerated resumes but actual imposters

entities.name: State-sponsored operatives
entities.context: Mentioned as a type of fake candidate applying to technical roles at startups

relationships.context: Resumes enable hiring for appearance rather than substance
Because appearance can be faked, fraudulent actors can pass resume screens
State-sponsored operatives are a subset of the fake candidate problem
The Fraud Problem is V's term for the fake candidate phenomenon
